title: coryphopteris horizontalis
intro: Coryphopteris horizontalis, also known as the Horsetail Fern or False Horsetail, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a diuretic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory, making it useful for treating conditions such as urinary tract infections and skin irritations. The health benefits of Coryphopteris horizontalis include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting the immune system, while also acting as a natural antiseptic to prevent infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a variety of ways to incorporate its medicinal properties into one's health routine.
